title: Velvety Peruvian Yukon Gold Potatoes Stir-fry
description: An earthy and velvety post-workout dish featuring Yukon gold potatoes, topped with crispy garlic toast for a satisfying and fodmap-friendly meal.
instructions: Wash and peel the Yukon Gold Potatoes, then cut them into bite-sized pieces.
Drain any excess water from the potatoes and place them in a bowl. Add salt, pepper, and oil, tossing well until coated evenly.
Place the potato pieces on an oven-safe baking sheet and bake for 20-25 minutes, turning once halfway through cooking time to ensure even browning.
While the potatoes are baking, slice the garlic thinly and place it on a separate plate to crisp up in the oven (toasted garlic).
Once done, remove from oven and let them cool slightly. In the meantime, heat the remaining oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the cooled potatoes to the skillet and stir-fry for about 5 minutes until they start getting golden brown and crispy on the edges.

Pour in the water or chicken broth, bring to a simmer, cover with a lid, and let it cook for another 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally until most of the liquid has evaporated and the potatoes are soft but not falling apart.
Add the sliced garlic (if desired), stir gently, then remove from heat. Serve hot over toast or enjoy as is.
